title: Podman fails to recognize HEALTHCHECK in certain BuildKit images
body:
### Issue Description

When pulling (some) images that were built with BuiltKit, podman fails to parse/store the declared healtcheck configuration of the dockerimage correctly. Tested with `docker.io/adguard/adguardhome@sha256:fc01fa555e6b324378176dff676c7791b2bb6ebfcf63bd2599db568287de7230`
This appears to be (almost) identical to the previously resolved issue #12226, possibly indicating a regression or incomplete fix.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```sh
podman pull docker.io/adguard/adguardhome@sha256:fc01fa555e6b324378176dff676c7791b2bb6ebfcf63bd2599db568287de7230
podman run --privileged --rm --name dinp --replace -d docker.io/docker
podman exec dinp docker pull docker.io/adguard/adguardhome@sha256:fc01fa555e6b324378176dff676c7791b2bb6ebfcf63bd2599db568287de7230
diff --color <(podman image inspect adguard/adguardhome | grep -i -A3 healthcheck) <(podman exec dinp docker inspect adguard/adguardhome | grep -A8 Healthcheck)
```

### Describe the results you received

```diff
<                     "created_by": "HEALTHCHECK &{[\"CMD\" \"/opt/adguardhome/scripts/healthcheck.sh\"] \"30s\" \"10s\" \"0s\" '\\x03'}",
<                     "comment": "buildkit.dockerfile.v0",
<                     "empty_layer": true
<                },
---
>             "Healthcheck": {
>                 "Test": [
>                     "CMD",
>                     "/opt/adguardhome/scripts/healthcheck.sh"
>                 ],
>                 "Interval": 30000000000,
>                 "Timeout": 10000000000,
>                 "Retries": 3
>             },
```

### Describe the results you expected

Podman should properly parse and store the healthcheck that is specified in the dockerimage.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

When pulling the image with docker, saving the image and loading it into podman, everything works as expected.
```sh
podman exec dinp docker save adguard/adguardhome | podman load
podman inspect adguardhome:latest | grep -A8 Healthcheck
```
```json
"Healthcheck": {
               "Test": [
                    "CMD",
                    "/opt/adguardhome/scripts/healthcheck.sh"
               ],
               "Interval": 30000000000,
               "Timeout": 10000000000,
               "Retries": 3
          }
```

### Image details:
adguardhome [Dockerfile](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdGuardHome/blob/e54fc9b1e9ad9f344edad86a2991308e6c5c29d0/docker/Dockerfile) , adguardhome [buildscript](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdGuardHome/blob/efed23701a505e43358e95ae3ea56fcc69a7bcf2/scripts/make/build-docker.sh)
```dockerfile
HEALTHCHECK \
	--interval=30s \
	--timeout=10s \
	--retries=3 \
	CMD [ "/opt/adguardhome/scripts/healthcheck.sh" ]
```

comments.body: Did you store it in Docker format?  I am not sure HEALTHCHECK is stored in OCI Format?

HEALTHCHECK is only a part of the Docker image spec and not OCI. The image in my example is in the Docker format.
#12239 introduced support for health checks from image configurations. However, in certain cases, `podman pull` fails to parse or store the health check configuration.
I think the problem is easier to understand when following the steps in Steps to reproduce and Additional information, that I provided above.

@salevdns As far as I can see https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/12239 only corrected to get Imageconfig for images when it is in `docker` format, and I think it works in case of `podman exec dinp docker save adguard/adguardhome | podman load` cause `docker save` is saving in `doceker` format.

Is expectation here is to check if `image` metadata somehow contains `Healthcheck` if yes, include it in `OCI`'s image's reserved field `Healthcheck` and `podman` must implement it while running the image ?
I am not sure tbh. To me it looks like it _should_ be stored in the docker format when pulling.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

In case it helps, I created a minimal test container in my accidentally reported duplicate issue:

* https://hub.docker.com/r/olifre/podman-healthcheck-test
  ( https://github.com/olifre/podman-healthcheck-test/blob/main/Dockerfile )

I observed the same problem pulling that one, and also the [official iobroker container](https://hub.docker.com/r/buanet/iobroker) which is built from [this Dockerfile](https://github.com/buanet/ioBroker.docker/blob/main/debian12/Dockerfile). Pulling the same containers with Docker and running them there reveals a working healthcheck.

FWIW, I started getting this with Kaniko some time ago for no apparent reason. Older versions of Kaniko+podman had healthchecks working normally, and, as expected, Kaniko+docker works without any problems. Forced me to switch back to docker for the time being :/

I found that images that use GitHub Actions with the docker/build-and-push action (v4 or greater) fail because they enable attestation by default, which I guess causes buildx to pick the OCI format for output. So if you control the build and use that action, a workaround is adding the option:

```yaml
- uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
  with:
    # ...
    provenance: false
```

Any update and/or workaround on this?

I'm hitting this issue with current Podman Desktop on macOS with an internal container (in Docker format):

`podman inspect my_Image:latest` correctly shows the health check configuration, but `podman inspect` on the running container shows no health checks!?

You can check the command by `podman inspect` and set this command on the command line or in your unit file. This is not really good. This issue appears to be more like politically blocked instead of technically blocked.

It's sad, previously podman was just updating to be compatible, now we are back to "this is not the right thing to do" approach which makes the project harder to use.

I'm hitting this issue with multiple images when using Quadlets.

For example:
``` bash
$ podman container inspect 81a586c454ed --format "{{json .State.Health}}"
null
$ podman container inspect 81a586c454ed --format "{{.Image}}"
efa24a0298d8046779c295510923c85a6be8b4c72c3ddef207fd6cd329b40b86
$ podman image inspect efa24a0298d8046779c295510923c85a6be8b4c72c3ddef207fd6cd329b40b86 | grep -i -A3 healthcheck
                    "created_by": "COPY scripts/healthcheck.py . # buildkit",
                    "comment": "buildkit.dockerfile.v0"
               },
               {
--
                    "created_by": "HEALTHCHECK &{[\"CMD-SHELL\" \"python3 healthcheck.py\"] \"0s\" \"0s\" \"0s\" \"0s\" '\\x00'}",
                    "comment": "buildkit.dockerfile.v0",
                    "empty_layer": true
               }
$ podman --version
podman version 5.3.2
```

After reading about this feature a bit, I understand it's not great to configure health checks in `Dockerfile` and that it's better to have them configured in `docker-compose.yml` or in the Quadlet `[Container]` section, However, the reality is that there are many existing images that rely on this functionality since it works fine in Docker, but it's currently silently broken in Podman.

Just adding that the workaround in the issue description (`docker pull`, `docker save`, `podman load`) does not appear to work for some images, including images built with `docker/build-push-action@v6` . This makes the main draw of `podman` for me, sd_notify for health checks, useless as I would have to duplicate all container health checks in the `podman run` command.
